title: stylidium leiophyllum
intro: Stylidium leiophyllum, also known as the needle-leaved triggerplant, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Stylidiaceae family native to Western Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Stylidium leiophyllum include its potential to treat wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be dried and used as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical remedy for various health concerns, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
